title: Counter State with ref
short_description: Practice declaring reactive primitive state with ref and updating it with .value.
description: In this challenge, you will complete a small counter component using Vue's ref function.
Focus on the first core rule from the lesson: inside <script setup>, refs are read and updated with .value, while the template can display them directly.
instructions: Declare a count variable using ref(0).
Make the increment function increase count by 1 using count.value.
Make the reset function set count back to 0 using count.value.
Display count in the template without writing .value.

hints.title: Start with state
hints.body: Inside <script setup>, import ref from vue and replace the plain value with const count = ref(0).

hints.title: Update a ref
hints.body: Inside functions, count is a ref object. That means increment needs to change count.value.

hints.title: Templates unwrap refs
hints.body: In the template you can write {{ count }}. Vue automatically displays the inner ref value.
